headline: Tech Rotation Swings Back Toward Software
summary: A trade that has punished software stocks in favor of semiconductors is reversing this week.  An iShares ETF tracking software stocks is up roughly 7% for the week, while another ETF tracking semiconductor producers has dropped about 5.7%—a more than 12-percentage-point gap on pace for the largest weekly software outperformance on record.  Software's beaten-down names such as ServiceNow, Intuit and Salesforce—which are each down more than 15% so far this year—were among the S&P 500's best-performing stocks Thursday.

headline: IGV Bounces 5% But the SaaS Selloff May Not Be Over Yet
summary: One of the more volatile tech funds, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSEARCA:IGV) has bounced 5.13% over the past week, reversing after a collapse that left the fund down 18% year-to-date from a December 31 starting price of $105.69. headline: Software Stocks Are Roaring Back
summary: The Nasdaq Composite is riding a rally in software stocks back into positive territory. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF, or IGV, rallied 2.6% after joining in on Wednesday's risk-on rally.

headline: Equilar ExecAtlas Partners with SalesIntel to Close Executive Data Gap
summary: REDWOOD CITY, Calif., March 05, 2026--Equilar ExecAtlas today announced a data integration with SalesIntel, a signal-first pipeline generation platform trusted by enterprise GTM teams. The partnership brings together trusted executive intelligence from ExecAtlas and AI + human-verified buying committee contact data from SalesIntel, enabling Salesforce users to maintain complete executive records, access verified contact information, and uncover warm introduction paths across key accounts.

headline: Beyond Alliance: Leading Companies will direct $100m to Cut Superpollutants
summary: A group of leading global companies has today announced a $100 million initiative to accelerate the reduction of superpollutants - powerful warming agents such as methane, black carbon, and refrigerant gases that are responsible for roughly half of all climate warming to date and can contribute to harmful air pollution.
